entries.statement: Knowledge of program management frameworks (PMI, SAFe, Agile) and how they are adapted for data and AI programs that involve technical uncertainty, iterative development, and cross-functional dependencies.
Knowledge of data and AI program risk types, including technical risk, data availability risk, model performance risk, adoption risk, and regulatory compliance risk.
Knowledge of change management frameworks (Prosci ADKAR, Kotter) and how to adapt them for data and AI capability deployments with heterogeneous user populations.
Knowledge of adult learning design principles, including learning objectives, instructional design methods, knowledge retention research, and blended learning delivery models.
Knowledge of data literacy concepts, including reading and interpreting charts, understanding data sources, recognizing misleading visualizations, and applying basic statistical reasoning.

Knowledge of DataOps principles and practices, including pipeline monitoring approaches, automated alerting, SLA management, incident response workflows, and operational metrics for measuring data pipeline health and reliability.

Knowledge of IT service management principles as applied to data services, including service catalog design, service level management, incident and problem management, and continual service improvement.

Knowledge of technology vendor evaluation methodologies for data and AI platforms, including RFP and RFI processes, proof-of-concept design, total cost of ownership analysis, and vendor risk assessment.

Knowledge of data and AI operational budgeting practices, including cost allocation models, chargeback and showback frameworks, cloud cost optimization strategies, and ROI tracking for infrastructure investments.
Knowledge of self-service data platform principles, including data marketplace design, self-service analytics enablement, data product discovery mechanisms, access provisioning, and usage monitoring.

Knowledge of professional development and technical currency practices for data and AI practitioners, including continuing education approaches, conference and community engagement, certification pathways, skills assessment methods, and strategies for maintaining relevance as tools, methods, and best practices evolve.
entries.strm_gap_source: STRM-001-ONET: O*NET 4.A.2.b.3 (Updating and Using Relevant Knowledge) — STS 0.421 to DG-S-008
